install.WARNING: The npm package name is EXACTLY '@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent'. Do NOT install 'pi' alone (wrong package — that's a Python utility). The CLI binary that lands on PATH is `pi`.
install.note: Pi is an open-source CLI coding agent by Earendil Works (MIT, 46k+ GitHub stars). It is multi-provider — supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Azure, Bedrock, Mistral, Groq, and OpenAI-compatible custom endpoints. Verify with `pi --version` after install. Config lives under ~/.pi/agent/ (auth.json + settings.json + models.json) — Echobird's 'Update model config' flow writes models.json + settings.json for you.

install_flow.summary: curl install or npm global → verify → Echobird writes ~/.pi/agent/models.json + settings.json on apply
agent_steps: macOS / Linux: prefer `curl -fsSL https://pi.dev/install.sh | sh` — handles binary download and PATH setup.
Windows: use `npm install -g @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` (verify Node.js ≥ 18 first; install via winget OpenJS.NodeJS / nvm if missing).
After install, run `pi --version` to confirm. If the command isn't found, instruct the user to restart their shell.

Tell the user that model selection happens via Echobird's App Manager — clicking 'Update model config' writes ~/.pi/agent/models.json (registers an 'echobird' provider) and ~/.pi/agent/settings.json (sets defaultProvider/defaultModel). Pi auto-reloads models.json on next /model command — no restart needed.
tell_user: Node.js prerequisite if Windows / npm path
That `~/.pi/agent/{models,settings}.json` is what Echobird writes when applying a model

identity_guard.description: Pi here means the open-source CLI coding agent by Earendil Works (pi.dev / github.com/earendil-works/pi). It is NOT 'Pi network' (the cryptocurrency app), NOT Inflection AI's Pi assistant, and NOT Raspberry Pi tooling — those are completely unrelated products that share the name. The npm package is `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` and the binary is `pi`.
